body.title: Fix 6 flaky tests — pe_entry_nodes (3) + uc_delivery_telemetry (3) only fail in full suite
body.result: 6 flaky tests fixed (commit c1fc0fb3). Two pollution sources isolated and patched: (A) pe_chain_debugger.start() loads real igor.switches.cfg into os.environ — fixed via autouse env snapshot/restore fixture in test_pe_chain_debugger.py; (B) Python 3.12 + asyncio.run() closes the default loop, breaking later asyncio.get_event_loop() calls — fixed by switching the 3 telemetry tests to asyncio.run(). Suite: 4301 → 4307 passing, 32 skipped, zero regressions.
body.description:
Six tests pass in isolation but fail when the full suite runs. Pattern is classic test pollution — shared module state, port reuse, or fixture leak from earlier tests.

**Failing tests (all green in isolation, all red in full suite):**
- tests/test_pe_entry_nodes.py::TestPeEntryInit::test_no_error_when_ticket_id_present
- tests/test_pe_entry_nodes.py::TestRunPeEntryChain::test_full_chain_populates_basket
- tests/test_pe_entry_nodes.py::TestRunPeEntryChain::test_full_chain_with_situate_uses_required_files
- tests/test_uc_delivery_telemetry.py::TestApiAgentSend::test_accepted_post_logs_info
- tests/test_uc_delivery_telemetry.py::TestApiAgentSend::test_empty_content_rejected_with_log
- tests/test_uc_delivery_telemetry.py::TestApiAgentSend::test_invalid_json_rejected_with_log

**Suspected pollution source:** Full-suite run shows OSError binding port 10143 ('address already in use') in test teardown — points at IMAPServer / utility_closet test fixtures not releasing port. uc_delivery_telemetry uses asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete() (DeprecationWarning), which can trip on a closed loop after another test's teardown.

**Akien's bar:** broken tests are ticketed AND fixed within 24 hours. Not a watch-and-wait — fix this sprint.

**Affected files:** tests/test_pe_entry_nodes.py, tests/test_uc_delivery_telemetry.py, possibly tests/conftest.py, possibly tests/test_utility_closet_server.py (likely culprit for port binding leak).

**Design rules:** test-pollution-isolation, no-event-loop-deprecation.

**Scope boundary:** IN — bisect the pollution source via per-file isolation runs, fix fixture/state leak so all 6 tests pass under the full suite. OUT — broader test-suite refactor; rewriting unrelated tests; fixing the audioop DeprecationWarning (separate ticket if needed).

**Test plan:** primary acceptance test is `python -m pytest tests/ -q` showing 4307 passed (was 4301). Bisection method: split tests/ into halves, find the polluting file via binary search; once isolated, reproduce with minimal pair, then fix the fixture leak.

**Why now:** Akien correction 2026-05-02: 'we don't leave broken tests. so that gets ticketed and fixed. does not have to be this instant, but will be done in the next 24 hours.'
